task_completion.description: Verify the 'Lead Vocal' track is created and starts at position 0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a track named 'Lead Vocal' in the Ardour timeline. It should contain an audio waveform region that aligns exactly with the very beginning of the timeline (position 0:00).

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Double Left' track is created, panned 100% Left, and its gain is reduced to approximately -6 dB.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a track named 'Double Left'. In its track header or the mixer window, the pan control should be turned entirely to the left (L), and the volume fader should be lowered to roughly the -6 dB mark.

task_completion.description: Verify the audio region in the 'Double Left' track is shifted forward (delayed) by approximately 20 milliseconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the timeline, the audio region for 'Double Left' should start slightly to the right compared to the 'Lead Vocal' region, indicating a small time delay.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Double Right' track is created, panned 100% Right, and its gain is reduced to approximately -6 dB.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a track named 'Double Right'. In its track header or the mixer window, the pan control should be turned entirely to the right (R), and the volume fader should be lowered to roughly the -6 dB mark.

task_completion.description: Verify the audio region in the 'Double Right' track is shifted forward (delayed) by approximately 35 milliseconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the timeline, the audio region for 'Double Right' should start further to the right than both the 'Lead Vocal' and the 'Double Left' regions, indicating a larger time delay.

task_completion.description: Verify the Ardour session has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Ardour window title bar; there should be no asterisk (*) next to the project name 'MyProject', which typically indicates unsaved changes. Alternatively, look for a 'Session saved' notification or the agent clicking 'Save' in the menu.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Ardour GUI to perform the mixing tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Ardour application open with visible mouse movements, menu interactions, or dialog boxes, rather than just terminal commands editing the session XML file.

integrity.description: Verify the actual vocal sample was imported and used.
integrity.visual_evidence: The regions in the timeline should display actual audio waveforms, confirming the file '/home/ga/Audio/vocal_sample.wav' was imported, rather than empty or blank regions.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use external scripts to bypass the manual UI setup.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show execution of Python or bash scripts designed to automatically modify the Ardour session file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The session must contain exactly these three tracks: 'Lead Vocal', 'Double Left', and 'Double Right'. The 'Double Left' track must be panned 100% Left, have its gain set to -6 dB (acceptable range -8 to -4 dB), and its audio region shifted forward by 20 ms (882 samples). The 'Double Right' track must be panned 100% Right, have its gain set to -6 dB (acceptable range -8 to -4 dB), and its audio region shifted forward by 35 ms (1543 samples). The shifts should be visually apparent in the timeline as slight offsets to the right relative to the Lead Vocal.
